architecture.summary: The system trains a cross-modal alignment model to embed images and captions in a shared space, then uses this model as a retrieval component to augment a multi-modal transformer for visual question answering. At inference, the system retrieves relevant image-caption pairs from an external knowledge source and incorporates them into the VQA model's context, with support for hot-swapping retrieval indices.

biblio.title: Cross-Modal Retrieval Augmentation for Multi-Modal Classification

claims.statement: the trained alignment model achieves substantial improvements in performance on image-caption retrieval w.r.t. similar methods
retrieval-augmented multi-modal transformers using the trained alignment model improve results on VQA over strong baselines
the approach supports novel applications for inference time such as hot-swapping indices
